lernziele: The course teaches the basics of autonomous mobile robots. Both hardware (energy, locomotion, sensors) and software (signal processing, control, localization, trajectory planning, high-level control) will be tackled. The students will apply the knowledge to program and control mobile robots. The module is divided into two sections, the first section handles ground robots: Differential drive vehicles, Ackermann steering vehicles and bi- and quadrupedal robots. The second section of the module focuses on uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs)

kurse.lernziele:
Attendees can:

  • Present current state-of-the-art mobile robot systems
  • integrate and use sensors, sensor systems to enable the autonomous behaviour of a mobile robot
  • explain and use perception and feature extraction modules necessary for the functionality of an autonomous mobile robot
  •  design and implement Kalman filter for sensor processing and navigation
  • integrate GNSS system for reliable outdoor localisation tasks
  • implement and evaluate navigation modules, including path planning, obstacle avoidance
  • explain and apply control architectures and robotic frameworks
kurse.planinhalt: The course teaches the basics of autonomous mobile robots. Both hardware (energy, locomotion, sensors) and software (signal processing, control, localization, trajectory planning, high-level control) will be tackled. The students will apply the knowledge to program and control mobile robots. The module is divided into two sections, the first section handles ground robots: Differential drive vehicles, Ackermann steering vehicles and bi- and quadrupedal robots. The second section of the module focuses on uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs).
